old_claim.note: Naval Logbooks provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Voyage reports documented sea trials, but record keeping was evidence from testing rather than a component needed to build the clock.

source_shape.source_locator: National Museums Scotland documents a purpose-made 1662 pendulum sea clock, its makers, sea trials, and failure as a longitude solution.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The artifact history supports mechanical clockwork as the prototype substrate while treating trial records and later rating practice as context.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected scope leaves this relationship outside the causal boundary. Voyage reports documented sea trials, but record keeping was evidence from testing rather than a component needed to build the clock.

ontology_before: marine_chronometer_prototypes treated naval_logbooks as a direct enabling relationship.
ontology_after: Voyage reports documented sea trials, but record keeping was evidence from testing rather than a component needed to build the clock.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: marine_chronometer_prototypes no longer has naval_logbooks as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A direct historical or technical source establishes naval_logbooks as a necessary causal input to the newly scoped marine_chronometer_prototypes claim.
short_reason: Voyage reports documented sea trials, but record keeping was evidence from testing rather than a component needed to build the clock.
